titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in Fayumic (GA 086/fa6)
British Library Or. 5707
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project
Christian Askeland

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (Christian Askeland and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made.

msDesc.surrogates: The images to which this transcription is linked are © British Library Board (BL Or. 5707); see further http://www.imagesonline.bl.uk

sourceDesc.additional: Walter E. Crum and Frederic G. Kenyon, “Two chapters of St. John in Greek and Middle Egyptian,” Journal of Theological Studies 1, no. 3 (1900): 415–433; Jacqueline Coppen and David Jacobs, “The conservation and visual enhancement of five Greek palimpsest manuscripts as part of the Rinascimento Virtuale project,” in Palimpsestes et éditions de textes: les textes littéraires, Actes du colloque tenu à Louvain-la-Neuve (septembre 2003), ed. Somers, Véronique, Publications de l’Institut Orientaliste de Louvain 56 (Louvain: Peeters, 2009), 123–160.

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by Christian Askeland for the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John in 2011.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file later converted to XML. Further information about the transcription procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition of the majuscules at
The XML specifications used were version 1.5 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Converted to XML specifications 1.5 by Hugh Houghton
Image links and page numeration corrected by Hugh Houghton
Converted to XML by Catherine Smith and Hugh Houghton

body.note: BL Or. 5707 f. 7a; foliation has been hypothetically reconstructed.

ab.note: Crum's transcription is problematic in this verse
Crum was able to decipher more text in this verse
Crum deciphered parts of two more lines
